item.title: Return of the Meme: 1642 (debris harry)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
Probably should have saved this for a bread opener but whatever.
That is definitely a Mexíca-coded Omen.
The new WWI themed warbond looks awesome. Hilariously, I called it a few months ago while gaming with friends: "wouldn't it be cool if they had a WWI-Themed Warbond?"
I need to get Pippin to be a Doordash driver to make us some extra money.
Speaking of Mesoamerican omens.
This yellowthroat wants to do research.
Shonto Begay: "View from Perkinsville Road"
Pippin tax Today's message from Pippin: "no." ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Even a stopped clock is right twice a day
item.dc:creator: CrimsonPermanentAssurance

item.title: And my visa BARELY arrived in time
item.description: First applied a little over 10 months ago. It's been a long process. Didn't feel real until just this morning as I looked over my barren apartment. Now my stomach is in knots. Initially, I regretted coming back from my one year stay in Japan back in 2024. But, looking back, it's been a blessing. I got to be part of my sister's wedding, I worked as a Senior IT person at the university I left to go to Japan the first time and met some incredible people (many of whom I'm staying in contact with), and I sorted myself out mental health-wise (thanks to the incredible people/coworkers, more on that later). Despite the dumpster fires going on in the US and my excitement to learn new things, make new friends, and experience a new side of Japan, I am reticent to leave. It's mostly anxiety trying to prevent me from breaking my comfy routine, so I'm doing my best to push past it. My job at the university didn't pay the greatest, but it was more than enough for me to live on my own and save up each month. I already know that if I were to stay, I would have regrets in just a year or two. Besides, I can always come back if things don't work out in Japan. But...would I even want to? I've got prospects in Europe (Ireland, Norway), so I've been toying with ideas there as potential backups. Let's wait and see how the first year(s) in Japan go first. Sorry for the stream of consciousness, it's the best way to get the anxiety thoughts out of my head. Look forward to more Japan pics and musings in the near future, if you're interested.
item.dc:creator: WyrdJP

item.title: Memes vol 641
item.description:
Jesus Christ dude
Restaurants that serve babies
I think heated rivalry is erotic. And really really good.
I hope to one day be as funny as this person
Bruce and Gertie tax. Hey guys I just wanted to let you know that Paulie died. He had a foreign body blockage and we couldn’t afford the surgery. I might post about it more someday but I just wanted to let everyone know
Nessie tax

item.title: inside of a catalina plane
item.description: those were german uboots terror during WWII. anti-sub, search & reco, and eventually transport plane. the plane has retractable/tile (don't know english word) gear, and can land on hard soil too. footages of the plane aren't rare. but good interior views are rarer
item.dc:creator: chichiFregiLover

And this time I had the guest appearance of Mr. Sunday! And a bit of a guest appearance by Miss Jellybean. You can watch the review here https://youtu.be/2B1Dwr29JXc
item.dc:creator: Skullwriter

item.title: I almost got squished by a run away semi
item.description: While waiting for the car in front of me to make the turn, i was getting a bit impatient. I just looked up to my rear view mirror and all of a sudden i saw Semi and Smoke. I had to GTFO then. Was still expecting the semi to clip me, but somehow he threaded the needle. Also, as people would ask, Audio Book is Brandon Sanderson's The Bands of Mourning. Book 6 is the Mistborn series. Love it!
item.dc:creator: CoopDH

item.title: GovBrief Today #411
item.description: Donald Trump threw a war and none of our friends came. With the entitlement of a petulant child, the president spent more than a year threatening and belittling America's traditional allies while publicly mocking their war dead. When the UK offered to help last week, he told them we didn't need anyone. Days later, with the Strait of Hormuz closed and oil above $95 a barrel, he asked every ally America has to send warships. Germany, France, Japan, South Korea, and the entire European Union all said the same thing in different languages: this is not our war, we didn't start it, and you didn't even consult us. Today he bravely posed for reporters and said the U.S. didn't need them anyway, although he did mention a couple of minesweepers would have been nice. The Navy decommissioned its last minesweepers in the Persian Gulf six weeks before the war started. They're sitting in a shipyard in Philadelphia. Fourteen months of "America First" bought America exactly what it paid for. And today, the drumbeats about Cuba echoed the ones we heard about Venezuela and Iran. Only Congress can stop this. Get on the phone and into your email and make sure that they do. We've emailed 6,600+ stories over 411 straight nights. The algorithm doesn't always show them to you. Subscribe now at govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com Today on the Website: https://go.govbrief.today/260317-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/joe-kent [2] https://go.govbrief.today/venezuela-wbc [3] https://go.govbrief.today/cuba-intervention [4] https://go.govbrief.today/embassy-security-review [5] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-mar17 [6] https://go.govbrief.today/texas-measles-136 [7] https://go.govbrief.today/florida-medicaid [8] https://go.govbrief.today/bondi-epstein-subpoena [9] https://go.govbrief.today/amazon-usps [10] https://go.govbrief.today/nj-prosecutor-rebuke [11] https://go.govbrief.today/judge-mocks-ballroom [12] https://go.govbrief.today/voa-reinstated [13] https://go.govbrief.today/ark-commandments [14] https://go.govbrief.today/nyc-subway-suit [15] https://go.govbrief.today/postal-funding [16] https://go.govbrief.today/pearl-jam Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos
